records.ety: [OE. schateren, scateren, to scatter, to dash, AS. scateran; cf. D. schateren to crack, to make a great noise, OD. schetteren to scatter, to burst, to crack. Cf. Scatter.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Shattered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Shattering.]

defs: To break at once into many pieces; to dash, burst, or part violently into fragments; to rend into splinters; as, an explosion shatters a rock or a bomb; too much steam shatters a boiler; an oak is shattered by lightning.
quotes: A monarchy was shattered to pieces, and divided amongst revolted subjects.

defs: To disorder; to derange; to render unsound; as, to be shattered in intellect; his constitution was shattered; his hopes were shattered.
quotes: A man of a loose, volatile, and shattered humor.
Shatter your leaves before the mellowing year.

defs: To be broken into fragments; to fall or crumble to pieces by any force applied.
quotes: Some fragile bodies break but where the force is; some shatter and fly in many places.

defs: A fragment of anything shattered; -- used chiefly or soley in the phrase into shatters; as, to break a glass into shatters.
